contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 10
- Description: Blog: link interactive artifacts + swap private Edgar repo for public dao_client reference
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_me_beta/pull/52
  
  Details:
  Improved the "Plug-and-play architecture: why every service reads from one sheet" blog post on truesight.me/blog so readers can actually click through to the interactive artifacts it describes, and so the technical references point at public code instead of a private repo.
  
  ## What changed
  
  - **Linked every interactive artifact** referenced in the post — previously these were plain `<code>` spans with no clickthrough:
    - DApp (`dapp.truesight.me`) including `create_signature.html`, `report_dao_expenses.html`, `report_sales.html`, `verify_request.html`
    - `agroverse.shop`, `oracle.truesight.me`, `truesight.me/beerhall`, `truesight.me/digital-signatures`
    - Main Ledger + Telegram Chat Logs Google Sheets (direct URLs to the shared workbooks)
    - Live JSON caches on GitHub raw: `store-inventory.json`, `dao_members.json`, `dao_offchain_treasury.json`
    - Edgar health-check endpoint (`edgar.truesight.me/ping`)
  - **Swapped private `TrueSightDAO/sentiment_importer` references for the public `TrueSightDAO/dao_client`** — the two existing links 404-ed for anyone outside the org; reframed the paragraph so readers are pointed at the public Python port that reproduces Edgar's submission protocol (RSA-2048 keygen, canonical payload, RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5 / SHA-256 signing, `POST /dao/submit_contribution`).
  - **Fixed the fabricated `dao_client` code example** — the snippet called `MembersCache.load()`, `.by_email()`, and `total_usd()` which do not exist. Replaced with the real API: `Contributors().for_public_key(...)` and `TreasuryCache.fetch().totals() / .items()`, verified against the source in `truesight_dao_client/cache/contributors.py` and `treasury.py`.
  - **Restructured the References section** into "Try it live" (interactive links) and "Source repositories (public)" so non-developer readers have an obvious starting point instead of a wall of GitHub URLs.
  
  ## Why
  
  - The post was selling "plug-and-play architecture" but offered no clickable entry points for a curious reader to actually plug in. Every named service is an interactive URL — they should all be live.
  - Pointing at a private repo (`sentiment_importer`) as the canonical Edgar source was a dead end for any public reader. The public `dao_client` package documents the same wire protocol and is the right canonical stand-in.
  - A broken code example in a blog post about "how to read from the cache" undermines the whole premise. Fixing it to compile against the real `dao_client` API makes the snippet copy-pasteable.
  
  ## Pull requests
  
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_me_beta/pull/52 — merged
- Contributor(s): Garyjob <garyjob@gmail.com>
- TDG Issued: 16.67
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
